title: boswellia dioscoridis
intro: Boswellia dioscoridis, also known as Salai guggal, is a plant species in the Burseraceae family, native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its resinous sap. The health benefits of Boswellia dioscoridis include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestive health, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Boswellia dioscoridis can be prepared as a herbal supplement, oil, or resin, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to various parts of the body.
